title: BCIT Basic Guard Duty
description: The BCIT Basic Guard Duty dataset comprises EEG, eye-tracking, and behavioral data collected during a sustained vigilance task in which participants verified replica identification cards against reference images. The study measured cognitive and physiological responses across varying task demands, with ID presentation rates ranging from 1 to 25 per minute. This dataset provides a rich resource for investigating EEG correlates of sustained attention, fatigue, and decision-making in realistic security screening scenarios.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using BioSemi 256-channel systems (plus 8 additional channels including 4 eye and 2 mastoid channels) at 1024 Hz sampling rate. Eye tracking was performed using Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250. Participants completed a guard duty task involving serial presentation of ID cards paired with reference face images, with self-paced two-alternative forced-choice responses (maximum 20s per trial). The task was divided into ten 5-minute blocks with ID presentation rates varying within blocks according to predefined profiles (1, 2.5, 10, 15, 20, and 25 per minute). Behavioral measures included accuracy and response times; subjective measures included Task-Induced Fatigue Scale, Karolinska Sleepiness Scale, and Visual Analog Scale of Fatigue.
